sglang.title: Fix FlashInfer GPU <-> CPU sync
sglang.author: thecodingwizard
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
There is an unnecessary GPU <-> CPU synchronization when running FlashInfer with tensor cores + page size 1. This is caused by moving last_page_len from the GPU to the CPU here. When the page size is 1, last_page_len is always 1, so we do not need to transfer this tensor from the GPU to t

eagle3 has a accept length over 2 but don't get a speedup TPOT than without eagle3. We found that the top_p_renorm operation is time-consuming in Eagle3 verify stage. Disabling this operation when the top_p parameter is not set can significantly improve the default Eagle3 decoding speed w

vllm.title: Do not use eval() to convert unknown types
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The qwen3_coder tool parser included a fallback to using Python's
eval() to attempt to convert an unknown type into a native Python
type. This allowed execution of arbitrary Python code, as long as you
could get that Python code to be passed as an argument to a tool call.
Fixes GHSA-79j6-g2m3-jgfw
S

vllm.title: [Optimization] Make new_block_ids None if empty
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Since our page size is 16, new_block_ids for decode requests is empty for every 15 out of 16 steps.
However, currently we serialize the empty lists every step.
This PR fixes this unnecessary overheads by making them None before serialization.

After https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/23246/files#r2288729414, I checked and found that some strings are continued on the next line using \ which causes unintended whitespace, and have opened this PR to fix them.
Example:
>>> def f():
...     a = f"abc d \
...     efg"
...     prin

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix Python 3.9 Support
vllm.author: jaredoconnell
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The purpose of this PR is to fix a change introduced in #22534
That PR used the pipe operator |, which only supports being used in place of union in Python 3.10 and newer. So this switches the file to use the python 3.9 supported method.
There was no indication that Python 3.9 was officially
